google._note: Each run writes to a tab named by date (YYYY-MM-DD). On rollover, older date tabs are merged into archive_tab and deleted; archive_tab and backlog_tab are preserved.

drive._note: Each job's PDFs are copied here (Drive Desktop auto-syncs). One folder per job per date.

schedule._note: One Windows Task repeats run_nightly.cmd from window_start every repeat_minutes until window_end. Self-retry: skips once runs/<today>/DONE exists; finishes leftovers after a token-limit reset. To change timing, re-register the task (see SETUP.md) or edit it in Task Scheduler.

search._sources_note: Each source fetches its own count. linkedin uses keywords (OR). indeed/xing use their own single-string query. arbeitnow is free (filters keywords locally). Disable any with enabled:false. Apify counts drive credit use.
search._note: fetch_count = jobs per pull (min 10). pulls_per_night>1 with spacing catches later-posted jobs; each pull costs ~$0.01 and dedupes so only new jobs get tailored. Tailoring resumes is FREE (no Apify cost).

recency._note: Newer = higher priority. <=1 day best, then <=3, then <=7. Older than hard_cutoff_days is dropped.

applicants._note: Drop jobs with >100 applicants. Fewer applicants ranks higher.

role_priority._note: Keep roles matching >=50% of profile. Among those, push PM/coordinator-type to top. favor_fields/avoid_fields to be configured later.

filters._watchlist_note: Companies to FLAG. Leave [] if none.
filters._volume_note: volume_cap = jobs fully tailored (EN+DE) per night. backlog_cap = next-tier jobs saved to the Backlog tab (link + score only, no resume) for review.
filters._weights_note: Composite score = match*w + applicants*w + recency*w, minus german_penalty if the job requires fluent German (candidate is beginner). Higher score = higher priority.
filters._profile_note: Used to estimate profile fit (management-with-tech target). Edit to retune what counts as a good match.
filters._clusters_note: Jobs are grouped into the cluster whose keywords they best match; each cluster reuses one shared resume. Order matters for ties (earlier wins). Edit freely.
